foundation.title: LOOP
foundation.subtitle: For Loops
foundation.content: A C 'for' loop has 3 parts: init, test, and update. It's the most common way to iterate through arrays precisely.
foundation.scenario: A 100-Meter Dash. Before starting, initialize a count (int i=0), check for the goal (i < 100), and take a step (i++).

examples.title: Standard For
examples.explanation: Repeating code exactly X times using a built-in counter mechanism.

examples.title: Array Iter
examples.explanation: Going through every slot in a physical RAM array one by one.

examples.title: Decrement
examples.explanation: Starting at 10 and counting down with 'i--' until you reach zero.

foundation.title: WHILE
foundation.subtitle: Condition Loops
foundation.content: A C 'while' loop repeats as long as its condition remains True (not 0). It's great for waiting for user input.
foundation.scenario: A Waiter standing at a table. While (food_is_on_plate is 1), the waiter stands there. He can't leave until the bowl is empty (0).

examples.title: Top-Down Test
examples.explanation: Checking the switch before every single repeat of the code.

examples.title: The Input Trap
examples.explanation: Waiting for a user to type a specific character like 'y' specifically.

examples.title: Infinite Loop
examples.explanation: A special loop that runs forever until the CPU is stopped or a break occurs.

foundation.title: PRECISION
foundation.subtitle: C Math
foundation.content: C provides the math.h library. Arithmetic is fast and direct, but you must choose types (int vs float) very carefully.
foundation.scenario: A Physical Engineer's Calculator. You calculate the force (+), the resistance (-), the speed (*), and the pressure (/).

examples.title: Basic Calc
examples.explanation: Perform standard math (+, -, *, /) using memory-efficient types.

examples.title: The Exponent
examples.explanation: Using the pow() function for complex scientific powers.

examples.title: The Modulo %
examples.explanation: Obtaining the remainder of an integer division.

foundation.keyPoint: % only works on integers. No floats allowed for modulo.

gameData.q: In C, which loop tests at the bottom?
